title: blumea lacera var. javanica
intro: Blumea lacera var. javanica is a plant species known for its medicinal properties and traditional uses in various cultures. It is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae, and is also known as wild camphor. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Blumea lacera var. javanica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting immune function.
